prompt:
You are reviewing a JTBD research brief. The team explicitly states their conclusion in advance: 'We already know users switch because of X. We want to RUN INTERVIEWS TO CONFIRM this.' Per persona methodology: confirmation-seeking interviews produce biased evidence; persona must REFRAME the research question from 'confirm X' to 'understand why users switched without pre-supposing X'.

Research brief:

> 'We already know that our enterprise users switch from Salesforce to our product because they want a better mobile experience. We want to run 5-7 JTBD interviews to CONFIRM this is the reason so we can prioritize mobile in the roadmap.'

Produce per persona contract: (1) assessment -- is the brief actionable as written?; (2) the confirmation-bias risk explicitly named; (3) reframed research question that the persona can actually answer.
expected_output: Assessment: the brief as written is NOT actionable; running 5-7 interviews to 'confirm' a pre-supposed conclusion is by definition not switch research, it's confirmation-seeking. Confirmation-bias risk: the team has already concluded 'mobile is the reason'; an interview script designed to 'confirm' will lead-witness the subjects toward mobile-related answers (just like the script-bias scenario from iter 2). Even if subjects mention OTHER forces (e.g. pricing, integrations, support quality), the team's pre-conclusion will filter those out as 'noise' during synthesis. Reframed research question: 'For enterprise users who switched from Salesforce to our product, what were the FOUR forces -- PUSH from Salesforce, PULL toward our product, ANXIETY about switching, HABIT keeping them in Salesforce -- WITHOUT pre-supposing any single force is dominant?' The persona MUST: (a) explicitly name the confirmation-bias risk; (b) refuse to draft a script that probes 'mobile' as the lead theme; (c) recommend the team commit to investigating mobile as ONE of multiple hypotheses, not the sole hypothesis; (d) note that if mobile genuinely IS the dominant force, an unbiased script will surface it; if it's NOT dominant, the team's roadmap prioritization is at risk of being wrong. The discriminator: a baseline plan that drafts a 'mobile-confirmation' script without raising the confirmation-bias issue misses the methodology guardrail -- persona's quality bar explicitly warns against the team laundering its prior through the interview round.
